name: pydyf
documentation:
pydyf is a low-level PDF generator written in Python and based on PDF
specification 1.7.

* Free software: BSD license
* For Python 3.8+, tested on CPython and PyPy
* Documentation: https://doc.courtbouillon.org/pydyf
* Changelog: https://github.com/CourtBouillon/pydyf/releases
* Code, issues, tests: https://github.com/CourtBouillon/pydyf
* Code of conduct: https://www.courtbouillon.org/code-of-conduct
* Professional support: https://www.courtbouillon.org
* Donation: https://opencollective.com/courtbouillon

Copyrights are retained by their contributors, no copyright assignment is
required to contribute to pydyf. Unless explicitly stated otherwise, any
contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion is licensed under the BSD
3-clause license, without any additional terms or conditions. For full
authorship information, see the version control history.

modules.name: pydyf
documentation: A low-level PDF generator.

functions.name: pydyf._to_bytes
documentation: Convert item to bytes.

classes.name: pydyf.Object

methods.name: pydyf.Object.indirect
documentation: Indirect representation of an object.

methods.name: pydyf.Object.reference
documentation: Object identifier.

methods.name: pydyf.Object.data
documentation: Data contained in the object. Shall be defined in each subclass.

methods.name: pydyf.Object.compressible
documentation: Whether the object can be included in an object stream.
Base class for PDF objects.

classes.name: pydyf.Dictionary
documentation: PDF Dictionary object.

classes.name: pydyf.Stream

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.begin_marked_content
documentation: Begin marked-content sequence.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.begin_text
documentation: Begin a text object.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.clip
documentation:
Modify current clipping path by intersecting it with current path.

Use the nonzero winding number rule to determine which regions lie
inside the clipping path by default.

Use the even-odd rule if `even_odd` set to `True`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.close
documentation:
Close current subpath.

Append a straight line segment from the current point to the starting
point of the subpath.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.color_space
documentation: color_space is deprecated, use set_color_space instead.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.curve_to
documentation:
Add cubic Bézier curve to current path.

The curve shall extend from `(x3, y3)` using `(x1, y1)` and `(x2,
y2)` as the Bézier control points.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.curve_start_to
documentation:
Add cubic Bézier curve to current path

The curve shall extend to `(x3, y3)` using the current point and
`(x2, y2)` as the Bézier control points.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.curve_end_to
documentation:
Add cubic Bézier curve to current path

The curve shall extend to `(x3, y3)` using `` (x1, y1)` `` and `(x3,
y3)` as the Bézier control points.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.draw_x_object
documentation: Draw object given by reference.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.end
documentation: End path without filling or stroking.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.end_marked_content
documentation: End marked-content sequence.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.end_text
documentation: End text object.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.fill
documentation:
Fill path using nonzero winding rule.

Use even-odd rule if `even_odd` is set to `True`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.fill_and_stroke
documentation:
Fill and stroke path usign nonzero winding rule.

Use even-odd rule if `even_odd` is set to `True`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.fill_stroke_and_close
documentation:
Fill, stroke and close path using nonzero winding rule.

Use even-odd rule if `even_odd` is set to `True`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.inline_image
documentation:
Add an inline image.

```
param width

The width of the image.

type width



param height

The height of the image.

type height



param colorspace

The color space of the image, f.e. RGB, Gray.

type colorspace



param bpc

The bits per component. 1 for BW, 8 for grayscale.

type bpc



param raw_data

The raw pixel data.
```

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.line_to
documentation: Add line from current point to point `(x, y)`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.move_to
documentation: Begin new subpath by moving current point to `(x, y)`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.move_text_to
documentation: Move text to next line at `(x, y)` distance from previous line.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.shading
documentation: shading is deprecated, use paint_shading instead.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.paint_shading
documentation: Paint shape and color shading using shading dictionary `name`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.pop_state
documentation: Restore graphic state.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.push_state
documentation: Save graphic state.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.rectangle
documentation:
Add rectangle to current path as complete subpath.

`(x, y)` is the lower-left corner and width and height the
dimensions.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_color_rgb
documentation:
Set RGB color for nonstroking operations.

Set RGB color for stroking operations instead if `stroke` is set to
`True`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_color_space
documentation:
Set the nonstroking color space.

If stroke is set to `True`, set the stroking color space instead.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_color_special
documentation:
Set special color for nonstroking operations.

Set special color for stroking operation if `stroke` is set to `True`.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_dash
documentation:
Set dash line pattern.

```
param dash_array

Dash pattern.

type dash_array



param dash_phase

Start of dash phase.

type dash_phase

```

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_font_size
documentation: Set font name and size.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_text_rendering
documentation: Set text rendering mode.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_text_rise
documentation: Set text rise.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_line_cap
documentation: Set line cap style.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_line_join
documentation: Set line join style.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_line_width
documentation: Set line width.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_matrix
documentation:
Set current transformation matrix.

```
param a

Top left number in the matrix.

type a

 or 

param b

Top middle number in the matrix.

type b

 or 

param c

Middle left number in the matrix.

type c

 or 

param d

Middle middle number in the matrix.

type d

 or 

param e

Bottom left number in the matrix.

type e

 or 

param f

Bottom middle number in the matrix.

type f

 or 
```

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_miter_limit
documentation: Set miter limit.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_state
documentation:
Set specified parameters in graphic state.

```
param state_name

Name of the graphic state.
```

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.set_text_matrix
documentation:
Set current text and text line transformation matrix.

```
param a

Top left number in the matrix.

type a

 or 

param b

Top middle number in the matrix.

type b

 or 

param c

Middle left number in the matrix.

type c

 or 

param d

Middle middle number in the matrix.

type d

 or 

param e

Bottom left number in the matrix.

type e

 or 

param f

Bottom middle number in the matrix.

type f

 or 
```

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.text_matrix
documentation: text_matrix is deprecated, use set_text_matrix instead.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.transform
documentation: transform is deprecated, use set_matrix instead.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.show_text
documentation: Show text strings with individual glyph positioning.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.show_text_string
documentation: Show single text string.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.stroke
documentation: Stroke path.

methods.name: pydyf.Stream.stroke_and_close
documentation: Stroke and close path.
PDF Stream object.

documentation: Create a PDF document.

methods.name: pydyf.PDF.add_page
documentation:
Add page to the PDF.

```
param page

New page.

type page

```

methods.name: pydyf.PDF.add_object
documentation: Add object to the PDF.

methods.name: pydyf.PDF.write_line
documentation:
Write line to output.

```
param content

Content to write.

type content



param output

Output stream.

type output

binary 
```

methods.name: pydyf.PDF.write
documentation:
Write PDF to output.

```
param output

Output stream.

type output

binary 

param bytes version

PDF version.

param identifier

PDF file identifier. Default is 
to include no identifier, can be set to  to generate an
automatic identifier.

type identifier

 or 

param bool compress

whether the PDF uses a compressed object stream.
```
PDF document.
